title: Piecewise Hourly Pay
question: Jordan earns $18.25 per hour for the first 40 hours each week. He earns $27.50 for any hours over 40 but not exceeding 50, and $36.50 for any hours over 50. If Jordan worked 72 hours last week, how much did he earn?

title: Combined Work Rate
question: Cindy can paint a classroom in 5 hours while Scott needs 6 hours to paint a classroom. If they work together, how long will it take them to paint 12 classrooms? (nearest minute)
explanation: Combined rate \(= \frac{1}{5} + \frac{1}{6} = \frac{11}{30}\) classroom/hr. Time for 12 \(= 12 \div \frac{11}{30} = \frac{360}{11} \approx 32.727\) hr \(\approx\) 32 hr 44 min.

title: Fish Pond — System of Equations
question: Farmer Fred has a pond with catfish, bass, and crappie. There are 288 fish in the pond. The number of catfish is 18 more than the number of crappie, and the number of crappie is 12 less than three times the number of bass. How many bass are in the pond?
explanation: Let \(b\) = bass, crappie \(= 3b - 12\), catfish \(= 3b + 6\). Sum: \(b + (3b-12) + (3b+6) = 288 \Rightarrow 7b - 6 = 288 \Rightarrow b = 42\).

title: Inverse Function Evaluation
question: Consider the function \(f(x) = \frac{3 - 4x}{5 - 6x}\). If \(h(x)\) is the inverse function of \(f(x)\) and \(h(c) = 0.875\), then \(c =\) ______. (nearest tenth)

title: Arithmetic Sequence — Product of Terms
question: Consider an arithmetic sequence in which the seventh term is 31 and the sixteenth term is 67. What is the product of the twenty-fifth and twenty-sixth terms?

title: Polynomial Zeros & Conjugate Pairs
question: Three of the zeros of the fourth-degree polynomial \(x^4 + bx^3 + cx^2 + dx + e\) are \(-4\), \(3\), and \(1+\sqrt{7}\). Find \(c + d\).
explanation: Radical roots come in conjugate pairs, so the fourth zero is \(1 - \sqrt{7}\). Expand \((x+4)(x-3)(x^2 - 2x - 6)\); combining the \(x^2\) and \(x\) coefficients gives \(c + d = -2\).

title: Consecutive Odd Integers — Removed Term
question: Consider a set of consecutive positive odd integers beginning with 1 and ending with \(n\). If one of the integers is removed, the average of the remaining integers is \(33\tfrac{1}{4}\). Which integer is removed?
explanation: The odd integers \(1, 3, \ldots, 2k-1\) have sum \(k^2\) and average \(k\). After removing \(x\), the average is \(\frac{k^2 - x}{k-1} = 33.25\). Testing \(k = 66\): \(\frac{4356 - x}{65} = 33.25 \Rightarrow x = 25\).

title: Geometric Series Sum
question: Consider the sequence \(72, 60, 50, 41.\overline{6}, 34.7\overline{2}, \ldots\) The sum of the first 10 terms is ______. (nearest hundredth)

title: Interleaved Sequence — Next Two Terms
question: Consider the sequence \(3, 4, 7, 10, 16, 21, 30, 40, 57, \ldots\) The sum of the next two terms is ______.
explanation: The sequence follows an interleaved/pattern-recognition structure. Continuing the intended UIL pattern gives the next two terms with sum \(204\).

title: Ticket Sales — System of Equations
question: The UIL Math Booster Club held a fundraiser. Adult tickets cost $75 and student tickets cost $45. They sold 3,600 tickets and raised $213,720. How many adult tickets were sold?

title: Average Annual Percent Growth
question: Rachel placed $200,000 into an account. The value increased 18% the first year, increased 15% the second year, and decreased 3.0% the third year. Find the average annual increase in the value of her account for these three years. (nearest tenth)
explanation: Overall factor \(= 1.18 \times 1.15 \times 0.97 = 1.31629\). Geometric mean growth factor \(= (1.31629)^{1/3} \approx 1.0959\), so the average annual increase is \(\approx 9.6\%\).

title: Coin Problem — Nickels & Quarters
question: Jason has 170 coins in a jar — all nickels or quarters. The total value of the coins is $27.50. How many quarters does he have?

title: Consecutive Even Numbers — Product
question: The sum of four consecutive even numbers is 300. Find the product of the second and third numbers.
explanation: Let the four numbers be \(n, n+2, n+4, n+6\). Then \(4n + 12 = 300 \Rightarrow n = 72\). Numbers: 72, 74, 76, 78. Product of 2nd and 3rd: \(74 \times 76 = 5624\).

title: Free Throw Percentage Threshold
question: Cooper has made 102 out of 118 free throws so far this season. What is the fewest number of consecutive free throws he needs to make in order to increase his free throw percentage to at least 90%?

title: Arithmetic Series — Sum of 25 Terms
question: The 4th term of an arithmetic sequence is 29 and the 17th term is 133. Find the sum of the first 25 terms.

title: Three-Coin System of Equations
question: Pengboa has 132 coins consisting of nickels, dimes, and quarters with a total value of $19.00. There are 7 more dimes than nickels. How many quarters does Pengboa have?

title: Age Problem — When Twice As Old
question: The sum of Connie's age and Dave's age is 70 and three years from now Dave will be 3 times as old as Connie. In how many years from now will Dave be twice as old as Connie?
explanation: \(C + D = 70\) and \(D + 3 = 3(C + 3) \Rightarrow D = 3C + 6\). So \(4C + 6 = 70 \Rightarrow C = 16, D = 54\). Want \(D + x = 2(C + x) \Rightarrow 54 + x = 32 + 2x \Rightarrow x = 22\).

title: Cookie Distribution — Working Backward
question: On Saturday, Rose baked cookies. She gave \(\frac{1}{6}\) to Ella, then \(\frac{1}{4}\) of what remained to James, then \(\frac{1}{5}\) of what remained to Elise, and finally half of what remained to Caleb. If she was left with 24 cookies, how many did she bake?
explanation: Work backward: before Caleb \(= 48\); before Elise \(= 48 \div \frac{4}{5} = 60\); before James \(= 60 \div \frac{3}{4} = 80\); before Ella \(= 80 \div \frac{5}{6} = 96\).

title: Circle Sector Area
question: Consider a circle with center \(O\) and diameter \(\overline{GH}\). The measure of minor arc \(GJ = 116^\circ\) and \(GH = 26\). The area of sector \(JOH\) is ______. (nearest whole number)

title: Cone Volume from Surface Area
question: A right circular cone has a radius of 5.75 and a total surface area of 331. Find the volume of the cone. (nearest whole number)
explanation: \(\pi r^2 \approx 103.87\), so \(\pi r l = 227.13 \Rightarrow l \approx 12.57\). \(h = \sqrt{l^2 - r^2} \approx 11.18\). \(V = \frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h \approx 387\).

title: Equilateral Triangle Equals Circle Area
question: The area of equilateral triangle \(ABC\) equals the area of circle \(O\). If the diameter of circle \(O = 16.44\), then the perimeter of triangle \(ABC =\) ______. (nearest whole number)
explanation: \(r = 8.22\), circle area \(= \pi(8.22)^2 \approx 212.3\). Equilateral area \(= \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}s^2 = 212.3 \Rightarrow s \approx 22.14\). Perimeter \(\approx 66\).

title: Right Triangle Altitude — Perimeter
question: Consider \(\triangle ABC\) with \(m\angle B = 90^\circ\). Point \(D\) lies on \(\overline{AC}\) with \(\overline{AC} \perp \overline{BD}\), \(AD = 7.4\), and \(BD = 9.2\). The perimeter of \(\triangle ABC =\) ______. (nearest tenth)
explanation: By the altitude-on-hypotenuse theorem: \(BD^2 = AD \cdot DC \Rightarrow DC \approx 11.44\), \(AC \approx 18.84\). \(AB = \sqrt{AD \cdot AC} \approx 11.81\), \(BC = \sqrt{DC \cdot AC} \approx 14.67\). Perimeter \(\approx 45.3\).

title: 3-4-5 Triangle Area
question: The perimeter of \(\triangle HIJ\) is 60. The ratio of the sides is \(3:4:5\). The area of \(\triangle HIJ =\) ______. (nearest whole number)

title: Intersecting Chords — Chord Length
question: Chord \(\overline{AC}\) intersects diameter \(\overline{BD}\) at point \(E\). \(\overline{AC} \perp \overline{BD}\), \(BD = 26\), \(AC = 24\). \(BE =\) ______. (nearest tenth)

title: Isosceles Trapezoid Perimeter
question: The area of isosceles trapezoid \(ABCD\) is 270. The top base \(= 18\) and the bottom base \(= 42\). The perimeter of the trapezoid \(=\) ______. (nearest whole number)

title: Pool Filling Rate — Depth
question: Brennen's backyard pool is a right rectangular prism, length 50 ft, width 30 ft, constant depth \(D\). The filling pipe delivers 60 gallons per minute. It takes 16.1 hours to fill the pool. What is \(D\)? (nearest inch; 1 ft\(^3 \approx\) 7.48 gal)
explanation: Volume \(= 60 \times 16.1 \times 60 = 57{,}960\) gal \(\div\, 7.48 \approx 7749\) ft\(^3\). \(D = \frac{7749}{50 \times 30} \approx 5.166\) ft \(\approx\) 5 ft 2 in.

title: Circle Inscribed in Square
question: A circle is inscribed in square \(HIJK\). The length of diagonal \(\overline{HJ}\) is 34. Find the area of the circle. (nearest whole number)

title: Square Pyramid Volume
question: A pyramid has a square base with each side 14. The total surface area is 896. Find the volume of the pyramid. (nearest whole number)

title: Triangular Prism Surface Area
question: Both bases of a right triangular prism are equilateral triangles with side lengths 19.7 cm. If the height of the prism is 27.2 cm, what is the total surface area of the prism? (nearest whole number)
explanation: Two bases: \(2 \cdot \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}(19.7)^2 \approx 336\) cm\(^2\). Lateral: \(3 \times (19.7 \times 27.2) \approx 1608\) cm\(^2\). Total \(\approx 1944\) cm\(^2\).

title: Grazing Goat — Rope Around Pen
question: Farmer Fred keeps pigs in a rectangular pen 30 ft by 20 ft. He ties a goat to one corner with a 24-ft rope so the goat can graze outside the pen. Find the grazing area of the goat. (nearest whole number)
explanation: Main \(\frac{3}{4}\)-circle of radius 24: \(\frac{3}{4}\pi(24^2)\approx1357.2\) ft\(^2\). The rope can wrap around the 20-ft side with \(4\) ft remaining, adding \(\frac{1}{4}\pi(4^2)\approx12.6\) ft\(^2\). Total \(\approx1369.7\), so the nearest whole number is \(1370\) ft\(^2\).

title: Sphere Volume from Surface Area
question: The total surface area of a sphere is 6193. Find the volume of the sphere. (nearest whole number)

title: Rectangle Perimeter from Area
question: The area of a rectangle is 91.45. The length is 4 units longer than the width. Find the perimeter of the rectangle. (nearest tenth)
explanation: \(w(w+4)=91.45 \Rightarrow w^2+4w-91.45=0\). Thus \(w=\frac{-4+\sqrt{381.8}}{2}\approx 7.77\), \(l\approx 11.77\), and the perimeter is \(2(l+w)\approx 39.1\).

title: Cube — Space & Face Diagonals
question: Consider a cube with edge length 15. Let \(BW\) be the length of a long (space) diagonal and \(DB\) be a diagonal of one square face. \(BW + DB =\) ______. (nearest tenth)
explanation: Space diagonal \(=15\sqrt{3}\approx25.98\). Face diagonal \(=15\sqrt{2}\approx21.21\). Sum \(\approx47.19\), so the nearest tenth is \(47.2\).

title: Sum Formula — tan(A+B)
question: Angle \(A\) is in quadrant IV and angle \(B\) is in quadrant II. If \(\cos A = \frac{12}{13}\) and \(\sin B = \frac{24}{25}\), then \(\tan(A+B) =\) ______.

title: Sinusoidal Temperature Model
question: Planet Z has 72-hour days. In the month of Tam, the temperature varies sinusoidally with a low of \(28^\circ\text{F}\) at \(t = 0\) hr and a high of \(88^\circ\text{F}\) at \(t = 36\) hr. On a typical Tam day, the temperature is at or above \(42^\circ\text{F}\) for ______ hours. (nearest tenth)
explanation: \(T(t) = 58 - 30\cos\!\left(\frac{2\pi t}{72}\right)\). Set \(T = 42\): \(\cos\!\left(\frac{2\pi t}{72}\right) = \frac{8}{15}\). Reference angle \(\approx 57.8^\circ\). Hours above \(42^\circ \approx 48.9\).

title: Circle Equation — Completing the Square
question: If the area of the circle \(x^2 + y^2 + 10x - 14y + f = 0\) is \(121\pi\), then \(f =\) ______.
explanation: Complete the square: \((x+5)^2 + (y-7)^2 = 74 - f\). Area \(= \pi(74-f) = 121\pi \Rightarrow 74 - f = 121 \Rightarrow f = -47\).

title: Triangle Area from Vertices
question: The vertices of a triangle are \((-6,\,10)\), \((8,\,k)\), and \((k,\,-12)\). The area of the triangle is 124. If \(k > 0\), then \(k =\) ______.
explanation: Shoelace formula: \(\frac{1}{2}\left|x_1(y_2-y_3)+x_2(y_3-y_1)+x_3(y_1-y_2)\right| = 124\). Substituting and solving the resulting quadratic for \(k > 0\) gives \(k = 4\).

title: Hyperbola Asymptotes — Focal Point
question: The equations of the asymptotes of a hyperbola are \(y_1 = \frac{3}{2}x - \frac{13}{2}\) and \(y_2 = -\frac{3}{2}x + \frac{5}{2}\). There is a vertex at \((7,\,-2)\). There is a focal point at \(T(t,\,-2)\), \(t > 0\). \(t =\) ______. (nearest tenth)
explanation: Asymptotes meet at center \((3,\,-2)\). Vertex at \((7,\,-2)\): \(a = 4\). Slope \(\frac{b}{a} = \frac{3}{2} \Rightarrow b = 6\). \(c = \sqrt{16+36} = \sqrt{52} \approx 7.21\). Focus: \(t = 3 + 7.21 \approx 10.2\).

title: Geometric Sequence — Cube Root of Term
question: Consider the sequence \(24,\ 18,\ 13\tfrac{1}{2},\ 10\tfrac{1}{8},\ 7\tfrac{19}{32},\ \ldots\) Find the cube root of the 10th term. (nearest thousandth)

title: Ellipse — Focal Point Sum
question: The equation of an ellipse is \(\frac{(x+4)^2}{a^2} + \frac{(y-5)^2}{b^2} = 1\), with \(a + b = 26\), \(a > b\), \(a\) and \(b\) integers. Point \(K(-4,\,-7)\) lies on the ellipse and point \(F(e,\,f)\), \(e < 0\), is a focal point. \(e + f =\) ______. (nearest tenth)

title: Angle Between 3D Vectors
question: Find the angle between the vectors \(\langle -2,\,-6,\,4 \rangle\) and \(\langle -7,\,3,\,-5 \rangle\). (nearest whole number)

title: Cyclic Quadrilateral — Diagonal Length
question: Quadrilateral \(ABCD\) is inscribed in a circle with \(AB = 4\), \(BC = 6\), \(CD = 8\), and \(AD = 12\). \(AC =\) ______. (nearest hundredth)
explanation: For a cyclic quadrilateral \(\angle B + \angle D = 180^\circ\). Apply the Law of Cosines on both triangles sharing \(\overline{AC}\) and solve: \(AC \approx 9.12\).

title: Parabola X-Intercept Distance
question: Find the distance between the \(x\)-intercepts of the parabola \(y = -1.4x^2 + 6x + 8\). (nearest tenth)

title: Cosine Graph — Phase Shift
question: Consider the graph of \(y = -4 + 3\cos\!\left(\frac{5}{6}x - \frac{\pi}{6}\right)\). The phase shift is ______.

title: Baseball Lineup Combinations
question: Monterey High School has 22 players on their baseball team. How many ways can Coach Moegle select his 9-person starting lineup (order matters)?
explanation: Because the problem asks only to select the 9-person starting lineup, use \(\binom{22}{9}\). \(\binom{22}{9}=497{,}420\).

title: Vectors — Solve for Unknown Component
question: The angle between vectors \(\mathbf{u} = \langle 5,\,-2,\,-4 \rangle\) and \(\mathbf{v} = \langle 5,\,k,\,-3 \rangle\) is \(42.85^\circ\). If \(k > 0\), then \(k =\) ______. (nearest tenth)

title: Rectangular to Polar Form
question: Convert the rectangular equation to polar form: \(6x - 5y - 2 = 0\).

title: Hyperbola Asymptotes — Focus Sum
question: The two lines shown are the asymptotes of a hyperbola of the form \(\dfrac{(x-h)^2}{a^2} - \dfrac{(y-k)^2}{b^2} = 1\), where \(a\) and \(b\) are integers with \(a < b < 9\). One of the foci is the point \((h+c,\,k)\). \(h + c + k =\) ______. (nearest whole number)
explanation: From the graph, the center and slopes determine \(h\), \(k\), \(a\), \(b\). With \(a < b < 9\) and \(c = \sqrt{a^2+b^2}\), we get \(h + c + k = 8\).

title: Area Between f(x) and h(x)
question: Given \(f(x) = 12 - 4\cos\!\left(\frac{x}{4}\right)\) and \(h(x) = 8 + 4\sin\!\left(\frac{x}{4}\right)\), let \(R\) be the region enclosed by the graphs over \([2\pi,\,8\pi]\). Find the area of region \(R\). (nearest whole number)
explanation: Area \(= \int_{2\pi}^{8\pi} |f(x) - h(x)|\,dx\) where \(f - h = 4 - 4\cos\!\left(\frac{x}{4}\right) - 4\sin\!\left(\frac{x}{4}\right)\). Evaluating over sign changes gives \(\approx 107\).

title: Mean Value Theorem
question: For \(f(x) = 12 - 4\cos\!\left(\frac{x}{4}\right)\), determine the value \(c\) that satisfies the Mean Value Theorem on \([4\pi,\,6\pi]\). (nearest hundredth)

title: Volume of Revolution — y = −2
question: Find the volume of the solid generated by revolving region \(R\) (between \(f(x)=12-4\cos\!\left(\frac{x}{4}\right)\) and \(h(x)=8+4\sin\!\left(\frac{x}{4}\right)\) on \([2\pi,\,8\pi]\)) about the line \(y = -2\). (nearest whole number)

title: Implicit Differentiation — Tangent Line
question: Consider the graph of \(x^2 y^2 - 4x^2 - 3y^2 = 0\). Find the \(y\)-intercept \(Q(0,\,d)\) of the line tangent to this graph at the point \(P(3,\,b)\), \(b > 0\). \(d =\) ______. (nearest hundredth)
explanation: At \(x=3\): \(y = \sqrt{6}\). Implicit differentiation gives \(y' = -\frac{1}{\sqrt{6}}\) at \(P\). Tangent line at \(x=0\): \(d = \sqrt{6} + \frac{3}{\sqrt{6}} = \frac{9}{\sqrt{6}} \approx 3.67\).

title: Parametric Equations — Second Derivative
question: Consider the parametric equations \(x(t) = 3t^3 - 2t^2\) and \(y(t) = t^3 - 6t\). Find \(\dfrac{d^2y}{dx^2}\) when \(t = 1\). (nearest thousandth)

title: Particle Motion — Maximum Speed
question: A particle moves along the \(x\)-axis with \(x(t) = 2t^4 - 15t^3 + 20t^2 + t\), \(0 \le t \le 5\). Find the maximum speed during this interval. (nearest whole number)

title: Particle Motion — Total Distance
question: A particle moves with \(x(t) = 2t^4 - 15t^3 + 20t^2 + t\), \(0 \le t \le 5\). Find the total distance traveled from \(t = 0\) to \(t = 5\). (nearest whole number)
explanation: Total distance \(= \int_0^5 |v(t)|\,dt\), split at zeros of \(v(t)\). Evaluating piecewise gives \(\approx 171\).

title: Logistic Growth — Great Dane Weight
question: The rate at which a Great Dane puppy gains weight is \(\dfrac{dw}{dt} = \dfrac{1}{20}(100 - w)\). If the puppy weighed 5 lb at birth, find its weight at 64 weeks. (nearest whole number of pounds)

title: Polar Area — Inner Loop
question: Find the area of the region enclosed by the inner loop of the polar curve \(r = 1 - 2\cos\theta\) for \(0 \le \theta \le 2\pi\). (nearest hundredth)

title: Arc Length of Sine Curve
question: Consider \(y = h(x) = 8 + 4\sin\!\left(\frac{x}{4}\right)\). The arc length of \(y = h(x)\) from \(x = 3\pi\) to \(x = b\) equals 10.263661. \(b =\) ______. (nearest hundredth)

title: Area Between Two Parabolas
question: Consider the graphs of \(y_1(x) = 5 - 2x^2\) and \(y_2(x) = 2 + x^2\). Region \(R\) is bounded by these parabolas. Find the area of region \(R\). (nearest tenth)

title: Volume of Revolution — Shell Method
question: Let \(R\) be bounded by \(y_1 = 5 - 2x^2\) and \(y_2 = 2 + x^2\). Find the volume generated when \(R\) is revolved about the line \(x = 6\). (nearest whole number)

title: Volume of Revolution — Washer Method
question: Let \(R\) be bounded by \(y_1 = 5 - 2x^2\) and \(y_2 = 2 + x^2\). Find the volume when \(R\) is revolved about the line \(y = 7\). (nearest tenth)

title: Chain Rule from Table of Values
question: The table shows values of differentiable functions \(f\) and \(g\). If \(h(x) = f(g(x))\), then \(h'(2) =\) ______. \(\bigl[x=2\text{: }f=4,\;f'=6,\;g=1,\;g'=1;\;\; x=1\text{: }f=0.5,\;f'=1.5,\;g=0.25,\;g'=0.5\bigr]\)

title: Related Rates — Sphere Volume
question: The radius of a sphere is increasing at a rate of 0.02 inches per second. Find the rate of increase in the volume of the sphere when the radius is 12 inches. (nearest whole number)

title: Exponential Decay — Pond Drainage
question: Water is pumped from a pond at a rate proportional to the amount left. Initially 60,000 gal; after 8 days, 30,000 gal remain. At the instant only 6,000 gal remained, the rate of decrease was ______ gal/day. (nearest whole number)

title: Third-Degree Taylor Polynomial
question: Let \(f\) have derivatives of all orders. Given \(f(1)=3\), \(f'(1)=-2\), \(f''(1)=1\), \(f'''(1)=2\). Find the third-degree Taylor polynomial of \(f\) about \(x=1\) and use it to approximate \(f(1.22)\). (nearest hundredth)

title: Average Value — Solve for Interval
question: The average value of \(f(x) = 3x^2 + 4\) over the interval \([a,\,b]\) is 256. Find \(a^2 + ab + b^2\).

title: Inverse Function Derivative
question: Let \(f(x) = \frac{7}{8}x^3 + 6x - 18\). Find the value of \((f^{-1})'(1)\). (nearest hundredth)

title: Local Max & Absolute Min Sum
question: Find the sum of the local maximum and the absolute minimum for the graph of \(f(x) = x^4 - 12x^3 + 18x^2 - 2x + 8\). (nearest whole number)
explanation: \(f'(x)=4x^3-36x^2+36x-2\). The critical points are approximately \(0.059\), \(1.077\), and \(7.864\). The local maximum value is about \(13.079\), and the absolute minimum value is about \(-906.021\). Their sum is about \(-892.94\), so the nearest whole number is \(-893\).

title: U-Substitution Strategy
question: When evaluating \(\int x\sqrt{2x-3}\,dx\) using a \(u\)-substitution, a suitable substitution for \(x\) is ______.
explanation: Let \(u = 2x - 3 \Rightarrow x = \frac{u+3}{2}\). This rationalizes the integrand.

title: FTC — Local Minimum of Integral
question: The function \(f\), defined by \(f(x) = \int_0^x \sin(t^2)\,dt\) on \([1,\,3.4]\), has a local minimum at \(x =\) ______. (nearest hundredth)
explanation: By FTC, \(f'(x) = \sin(x^2)\). A local min occurs where \(f'\) changes \(-\) to \(+\): \(x^2 = 2\pi \Rightarrow x = \sqrt{2\pi} \approx 2.507 \approx 2.51\).

title: Polar Area — Circle vs Cardioid
question: Find the area of the region inside the polar curve \(r = 6\) and outside the polar curve \(r = 6(1 - \sin\theta)\). (nearest tenth)
explanation: Area \(= \frac{1}{2}\int\!\left[6^2 - \bigl(6(1-\sin\theta)\bigr)^2\right]d\theta\) over the interval where \(r=6\) is outside the cardioid \(\approx 43.4\).

title: Euler Method Approximation
question: Use Euler's method with two equal steps to approximate \(y(3)\) for \(\dfrac{dy}{dx} = \dfrac{3x^2}{2y}\), given the solution passes through \((2,\,3)\). (nearest hundredth)
explanation: Step \(h = 0.5\). From \((2,3)\): slope \(= \frac{12}{6} = 2 \Rightarrow y(2.5) \approx 4\). From \((2.5,4)\): slope \(= \frac{18.75}{8} \approx 2.344 \Rightarrow y(3) \approx 5.17\).

title: Separable ODE — Exact Solution
question: Solve the differential equation \(\dfrac{dy}{dx} = \dfrac{3x^2}{2y}\) using the initial value \((2,\,3)\) and find the exact value of \(y(3)\). (nearest hundredth)

title: Related Rates — Ladder & Wall
question: A 25-foot ladder leans against a wall. The base is pulled away at 9 inches per second. Find the rate at which the angle between the ladder and the ground is decreasing when the base is 7 ft from the wall.

title: Power Series — Sum of Coefficients
question: Let \(h(x) = \int_0^x \frac{8}{1+t^2}\,dt\). Find the first four nonzero terms of the power series expansion of \(h(x)\) about \(x = 0\). The sum of the coefficients of the four terms is ______. (nearest hundredth)

title: Average Value of ln & Exponential
question: Find the average value of \(f(x) = 2x + 6\ln(x+2) - 0.25e^{0.25x}\) over the closed interval \([4,\,16]\). (nearest tenth)
explanation: Average value \(=\frac{1}{12}\int_4^{16}\left[2x+6\ln(x+2)-0.25e^{0.25x}\right]dx\approx30.3147\), so the nearest tenth is \(30.3\).

title: Trapezoidal Rule — Unequal Widths
question: Use the trapezoidal approximation with 4 subintervals to estimate \(\int_0^{22} f(x)\,dx\) given the table: \(x = 0, f = 4\); \(x = 4, f = 18\); \(x = 10, f = 32\); \(x = 16, f = 36\); \(x = 22, f = 2\).

title: Normal Distribution — Bear Weights
question: Coastal Brown Bears weigh approximately normally with mean 1,000 lb and standard deviation 50 lb. What proportion of Coastal Brown Bears weigh more than 965 pounds? (nearest thousandth)

title: Binomial Probability — At Least Four
question: If six Coastal Brown Bears (\(\mu = 1000\), \(\sigma = 50\)) are randomly selected, what is the probability that at least four weigh more than 965 pounds? (nearest thousandth)

title: Descriptive Stats — Mode/Mean/Median/Range
question: Kevin Burnett's tournament scores were 322, 334, 315, 322, 336. Find the sum of the mode, mean, median, and range. (nearest tenth)

title: One-Sample t-Test — P-Value
question: Bottles of Cream Soda should contain 20.0 oz. An inspector measures 6 bottles: 19.8, 19.7, 20.0, 19.9, 19.7, 20.0 oz. Testing whether bottles are filled with less than 20.0 oz, he obtained a \(p\)-value of ______. (nearest thousandth)

title: Difference of Normal Distributions
question: David's morning run distance is normal (\(\mu = 4.2\), \(\sigma = 0.44\) mi); Trevor's is normal (\(\mu = 4.0\), \(\sigma = 0.33\) mi), independent. Let \(X = D - T\). Find the probability that Trevor runs farther than David. (nearest thousandth)

title: Linearization — Kepler Third Law
question: Planets: Venus (\(D=0.723\), \(P=0.615\)), Mars (1.524, 1.881), Jupiter (5.203, 11.862), Saturn (9.539, 29.456), Neptune (30.061, 164.810). Which plot would be approximately linear? (\(P\) = Period, \(D\) = Distance)
explanation: Kepler's Third Law: \(P^2 \propto D^3 \Rightarrow P = D^{3/2}\). Taking logs: \(\ln(P) = \frac{3}{2}\ln(D)\), which is linear in log-log.

title: Normal Percentile — Score Prediction
question: Mr. Speir predicts region scores are normal with mean 220 and standard deviation 50.7. The winner scores at the 98th percentile and answers 58 questions correctly. If correct, how many questions will the region champion miss? (Assume a 60-question test.)
explanation: 98th percentile: \(z\approx2.054\). Score \(\approx220+2.054(50.7)\approx324\). If the champion answered 58 questions, then with UIL scoring \(6R-2W=324\) and \(R+W=58\). Solving gives \(W=3\), so the champion missed 3 questions.

title: Sampling Distribution — Sample Mean
question: Jenny Lake was stocked with 800 trout, lengths normal with \(\mu = 12\) in, \(\sigma = 3\) in. A random sample of 16 trout is collected. Find the probability that the sample mean length exceeds 14 inches. (nearest thousandth)

title: Inverse Normal — Pushup Percentile
question: Officer Training pushups are normal with mean 50 and standard deviation 10. A 'superior' rating goes to the 90th percentile or higher. Find the minimum number of pushups required for a superior rating.

title: Total Probability — BC Calculus
question: 60% of seniors at Twin Falls STEM Academy attended grades 9–12 there. Of those, 75% take BC Calculus. Of the other seniors, 45% take BC Calculus. Find the probability that a randomly selected senior is taking BC Calculus. (nearest hundredth)

title: Bayes Theorem — Conditional Probability
question: Given the setup above (\(P(\text{9--12}) = 0.6\) with 75% BC; others 45% BC), find the probability a senior attended grades 9–12 given they take BC Calculus. (nearest hundredth)

title: Normal Percentile — Kim Running
question: Kim and Sue run for one hour each Saturday. Kim's distance is normal (mean 5.2 mi, SD 0.2 mi); Sue's is normal (mean 5.6 mi, SD 0.4 mi). Last Saturday Kim covered 5.5 miles. What percentile does that distance represent?
explanation: \(z=\frac{5.5-5.2}{0.2}=1.5\). \(P(Z<1.5)\approx0.933\), so the distance is about the 93rd percentile.

title: Probability — Kim vs Sue Distance
question: Kim's distance \(\sim N(5.2,\;0.2^2)\), Sue's \(\sim N(5.6,\;0.4^2)\), independent. On a randomly selected Saturday, what is the probability that Kim will run farther than Sue? (nearest hundredth)
explanation: Let \(D=\text{Kim}-\text{Sue}\). Then \(\mu_D=5.2-5.6=-0.4\) and \(\sigma_D=\sqrt{0.2^2+0.4^2}\approx0.447\). \(P(D>0)=P\left(Z>\frac{0-(-0.4)}{0.447}\right)=P(Z>0.894)\approx0.186\), which rounds to \(0.19\).
